title: glechoma mollis
intro: Glechoma mollis, also known as Creeping Germander or Ground Ivy, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its spreading stems and small, fragrant leaves. Traditionally, it has been used in medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Glechoma mollis include its ability to relieve digestive issues, soothe respiratory problems, and boost the immune system, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. In herbal preparations, Glechoma mollis is often combined with other herbs or used as a tincture, tea, or infusion to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
